dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal implements bidirectional trend-following logic. Entry requires price crossing 20-period MA, ADX confirmation (>18), and directional confirmation via prior close comparison as proxy for '3 prior closes' requirement (exact multi-bar comparison not supported). Exit uses MA crossover reversal and ADX weakening (<15). ADX_14 used as the only available ADX indicator. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to enable both long and short entries based on trend direction.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal implements bidirectional trend-following logic. Entry requires price crossing 20-period MA, ADX confirmation (>18), and directional confirmation via prior close comparison as proxy for '3 prior closes' requirement (exact multi-bar comparison not supported). Exit uses MA crossover reversal and ADX weakening (<15). ADX_14 used as the only available ADX indicator. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to enable both long and short entries based on trend direction.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter positions aligned with trend direction because trend-following systems never miss major market moves, and price distributions exhibit long tails in the direction of sustained trends rather than random walk behavior.
